_comment: NPC: Blood Ancestor (Yao Xinghai) (血祖 / 姚星海). Canon ID: N98. Per user directive: every miscellaneous NPC from RI must be accounted for. Prime Directive: Reality is objective; cultivation changes understanding, not existence. Every being described here exists independently of the player's perception.
name: Blood Ancestor (Yao Xinghai)
relationship_to_wanglin: major enemy; Wang Lin kills him

perception_tiers.nascent_soul: Peak Third Step; blood lineage incarnate
perception_tiers.spirit_severing: Peak Third Step; can reform from blood sea

note: Blood Ancestor; major antagonist Wang Lin kills

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi. Per canon: Wang Lin's Dao heart is famously tested at every major realm.

initiation_lines: Blood is the original cultivation resource. Before spirit stones, before herbs, before jade slips — there was blood. The Yao Family remembers this. The Yao Family practices this. The Yao Family IS this.
Wang Lin killed me. Or rather, he killed this body. The Blood Sea reforms. The Blood Ancestor returns. Death is a temporary inconvenience for one who has mastered the art of blood reincarnation.

The Soul Refining Sect refines souls. The Corpse Yin Sect animates corpses. Both are crude imitations of what the Blood Sea accomplishes naturally. Blood carries memory, power, lineage. Everything else is a shadow of blood's truth.

Redstone and bone meal. The Blood Sea requires these to sustain its territorial expansion. Bring them, and I will teach you the first form of Blood Reincarnation. What you do with that knowledge is your own affair.

daily_schedule.action: Absorb blood energy from the sea

sect_tasks.name: Blood Sea Expansion Materials
sect_tasks.description: The Blood Ancestor needs redstone and bone meal to sustain and expand the Blood Sea's territorial boundary.

sect_tasks.name: Blood Reincarnation Knowledge
sect_tasks.description: The Blood Ancestor trades forbidden Blood Reincarnation arts in exchange for soul fragments and jade slips documenting other cultivation methods.
